summary: Ukrainian forces struck an oil depot and a spent nuclear fuel storage building in occupied Crimea and Kyiv region, while Russian forces intensified UAV and rocket attacks across multiple fronts, including a drone strike that halted the Moscow‑Simferopol train and a deadly drone assault on Zaporizhzhia. Russian tactical aviation activity increased in the north‑east and south‑east, prompting heightened air‑defence alerts. Drone operations continued over Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and other oblasts, with Shahed missiles reported near Lublin and Barishivka.
key_points: Ukrainian special‑operations units hit the Semikolo‑dezyansʹka oil depot and a fuel terminal in occupied Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/81749).
A Russian drone attack damaged a building of the Centralized Storage of Spent Nuclear Fuel in Kyiv region; radiation levels remained normal (Source: @UkraineNow/81746).
A drone strike on the Moscow‑Simferopol passenger train caused suspension of rail traffic in Crimea and evacuation of passengers (Source: @tass_agency/379727).
Russian drones attacked Zaporizhzhia, igniting a fire, killing three people and injuring a woman, with damage to private houses (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on the Chonhar bridge to Crimea, leading to its closure and logistical difficulties for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on north‑east and south‑east axes, with warnings of aerial attacks on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64396).
Shahed‑type missiles were observed heading toward Lublin, Barishivka, and other locations, indicating continued missile‑drone threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67522).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian special‑operations struck an oil depot and a spent nuclear fuel storage building in occupied Crimea and Kyiv region, while Russian forces intensified UAV and rocket attacks across multiple fronts, including a drone strike that halted the Moscow‑Simferopol train and a deadly drone assault on Zaporizhzhia. Russian tactical aviation activity increased in the north‑east and south‑east, prompting heightened air‑defence alerts. Drone operations continued over Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and other oblasts, with Shahed missiles reported near Lublin and Barishivka.

points: Ukrainian special‑operations units struck the Semikolo‑dezyansʹka oil depot and a fuel terminal in occupied Crimea, damaging Russian logistical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81749).
A Ukrainian strike damaged the building of the Centralized Storage of Spent Nuclear Fuel in Kyiv region; authorities reported no radiological release (Source: @UkraineNow/81746).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on the Chonhar bridge leading to its closure, forcing Russian logistics to seek alternative routes (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
Ukrainian intelligence noted multiple Shahed missile trajectories toward Lublin and Barishivka, indicating ongoing offensive operations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67522).
Ukrainian reports listed UAV courses from Sumy, Kharkiv, and Dnipropetrovsk regions targeting Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/64424).
Russian forces used tactical aviation on north‑east and south‑east axes, warning of possible air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64396).
Russian rockets (KAB) were launched toward eastern Kharkiv, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64409).
A Russian drone attack on a passenger train Moscow‑Simferopol forced suspension of rail service in Crimea and evacuation of passengers (Source: @tass_agency/379727).
Russian UAVs attacked Zaporizhzhia, causing a fire, three civilian deaths, and injuries to a woman, with damage to private houses (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Russian forces reported a drone strike on a building of the Centralized Storage of Spent Nuclear Fuel, though radiation remained within limits (Source: @UkraineNow/81746).
UAVs from the north of Sumy oblast were reported heading toward Sumy, Okhtyrka, Shostka, and Krivelets (Source: @kpszsu/64420).
Shahed missiles were observed heading toward Pavlohrad, Kryvyi Rih, and Solone in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/64424).
Russian rockets targeted eastern Kharkiv and Donetsk oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64409).
UAV activity was noted over the north of Kharkiv heading to Chuhuiv (Source: @kpszsu/64424).
Ukrainian forces reported UAVs from the north of Kharkiv heading to Bogoduhiv (Source: @kpszsu/64416).
UAVs from northern Sumy were reported heading to Shostka and Konotop (Source: @kpszsu/64407).
UAVs from northern Rivne region were heading toward Sarna and Dubrovytsia (Source: @kpszsu/64416).
UAVs from northern Zhytomyr were heading toward Rivne and Sarna (Source: @kpszsu/64415).
A group of UAVs from Bryansk region (Russia) was reported heading north into Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/64399).
UAVs from northern Kharkiv were observed heading toward Chuhuiv (Source: @kpszsu/64424).
UAVs from northern Kherson were heading north toward Dnipropetrovsk and Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/64405).
UAVs from southern Sumy were heading toward Kirikivka, Boromlya, and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/64425).
UAVs from the north of Kherson were also reported heading toward Dnipropetrovsk and Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/64403).
UAVs from the Black Sea were heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64416).
UAVs from the north of Zaporizhzhia were reported heading southward (Source: @kpszsu/64425).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on north‑east and south‑east fronts, with warnings of aerial attacks on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64396).
Ukrainian Shahed missiles were observed over Lublin and Barishivka, indicating continued missile‑drone threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67522).
Russian rockets (KAB) were launched toward eastern Kharkiv, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64409).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over multiple oblasts, including Sumy, Kharkiv, and Dnipropetrovsk, targeting Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/64424).
A Russian drone attack on a passenger train in Crimea caused evacuation and suspension of rail traffic (Source: @tass_agency/379727).

key_developments: Ukrainian special‑operations struck the Semikolo‑dezyansʹka oil depot and a fuel terminal in occupied Crimea, damaging Russian logistical capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81749).
A Russian drone attack damaged a building of the Centralized Storage of Spent Nuclear Fuel in Kyiv region; radiation levels remained within normal limits (Source: @UkraineNow/81746).
A drone strike on the Moscow‑Simferopol passenger train forced suspension of rail service in Crimea and evacuation of all passengers (Source: @tass_agency/379727).
Russian drones attacked Zaporizhzhia, causing a fire, three civilian deaths, and injuries to a woman, with damage to private houses (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on the Chonhar bridge to Crimea, leading to its closure and logistical challenges for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on north‑east and south‑east axes, warning of possible aerial attacks on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64396).
Shahed‑type missiles were observed heading toward Lublin and Barishivka, indicating continued missile‑drone threats against Ukrainian targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67522).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and rocket activity remains high across the eastern, northern, and southern fronts, targeting infrastructure and civilian areas, while Ukrainian special‑operations continue to strike high‑value logistics sites in occupied Crimea. The drone strike on the Moscow‑Simferopol train demonstrates Russia's vulnerability to aerial attacks deep in its rear areas. Continued Shahed missile launches pose a persistent threat to Ukrainian air‑defence and critical infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have heightened air‑defence alerts and reported increased civilian casualties, especially in Zaporizhzhia and Crimea. Russian forces have suspended rail traffic in Crimea and are seeking alternative logistics routes after the Chonhar bridge closure. International observers note the escalation of UAV warfare as a key factor shaping front‑line dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows coordinated attacks from multiple directions, including cross‑border incursions from Russian‑held territories. The use of Shahed missiles near Lublin suggests possible spill‑over or mis‑targeting, requiring vigilance. The successful Ukrainian strikes on fuel and nuclear storage facilities indicate improved strike capabilities against hardened targets.
